Linked List

0) Concept

  • fucking algorithm : reverse part of linked list

  • fucking algorithm : reverse k set of linked list

  • fucking algorithm : check palindrome linked list

  • Use “pseudo head node” 虛擬頭節點

    • 代碼隨想錄: LC 203 Remove Linked List Elements

  • When delete node from linked list, we need to be at “previous” node, then can delete NEXT node

    • so, need to be at cur node, than can cur.next node
    1# python
2# https://youtu.be/Y4oQJklHxVo?t=965
3cur.next = cur.next.next

1# python
2# Definition for singly-linked list.
3class ListNode(object):
4    def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
5        self.val = val
6        self.next = next

1// java
2
3// Single Linkedlist
4
5public class ListNode{
6
7    // attr
8    public int val;
9    public ListNode next;
10
11    // constructor
12    public ListNode(){
13
14    }
15
16    public ListNode(int val){
17        this.val = val;
18    }
19
20    ListNode(int val, ListNode next){
21        this.val = val;
22        this.next = next;
23    }
24
25}
26
27// init a ListNode
28ListNode node1 = new ListNode(1);
29ListNode node2 = new ListNode(2);
30ListNode node3 = new ListNode(3);
31
32// motify node's value
33node1.val = 0;
34
35// connect nodes
36node1.next = node2;
37node2.next = node3;

1// java
2
3// Double linked list
4
5// LC 146
6
7public class Node {
8    int key;
9    int val;
10    Node prev;
11    Node next;
12
13    public Node(int key, int val) {
14        this.key = key;
15        this.val = val;
16        this.prev = null;
17        this.next = null;
18    }
19}

  • Linked list
  • Cycle linked list
  • Bi-direction linked list

    • LC 146

    • LC 138 :
    1dic = dict()
2m = n = head
3dic[m] = Node(m.val)
    • LC 208 :
    • trie
    1self.children = defaultdict(Node)
  • problem types

      • reverse linked list
        • LC 206
      • reverse linked list within start, end point

      • reverse part of linked list
      • reverse k set of linked list

      • merge 2 linked list

      • check cyclic linked list
      • check beginning of cyclic linked list

      • Remove Nth Node From End of List - LC 19
    • combinations
      • combinations of above cases

0-2) Pattern

Dummy Head Technique

Definition: Create a dummy/pseudo head node that points to the actual head, making it easier to handle edge cases and node removal operations.

When to Use:

  • Removing nodes from the beginning of the list
  • When the head node might be modified
  • Simplifying edge case handling
  • Operations that need to track the previous node

Time Complexity: O(n) - same as without dummy head Space Complexity: O(1) - only one extra node

Template Pattern:

1def linked_list_operation(head):
2    # Create dummy head
3    dummy = ListNode(0)
4    dummy.next = head
5
6    # Use prev to track previous node
7    prev = dummy
8    curr = head
9
10    while curr:
11        # Perform operations
12        if condition:
13            # Remove current node
14            prev.next = curr.next
15        else:
16            prev = curr
17        curr = curr.next
18
19    # Return new head (dummy.next)
20    return dummy.next

Advantages:

  • Eliminates need for special handling of head node
  • Simplifies code logic
  • Reduces edge case bugs
  • Consistent prev pointer throughout traversal

Common Use Cases:

1. Remove Nth Node From End (LC 19):

1def removeNthFromEnd(self, head, n):
2    dummy = ListNode(0)
3    dummy.next = head
4    fast = slow = dummy
5
6    # Move fast n+1 steps ahead
7    for _ in range(n + 1):
8        fast = fast.next
9
10    # Move both until fast reaches end
11    while fast:
12        fast = fast.next
13        slow = slow.next
14
15    # Remove nth node
16    slow.next = slow.next.next
17    return dummy.next

2. Remove Duplicates (LC 83):

1def deleteDuplicates(self, head):
2    dummy = ListNode(0)
3    dummy.next = head
4    prev = dummy
5
6    while head and head.next:
7        if head.val == head.next.val:
8            # Skip all duplicates
9            val = head.val
10            while head and head.val == val:
11                head = head.next
12            prev.next = head
13        else:
14            prev = head
15            head = head.next
16
17    return dummy.next

3. Merge Two Sorted Lists (LC 21):

1def mergeTwoLists(self, l1, l2):
2    dummy = ListNode(0)
3    current = dummy
4
5    while l1 and l2:
6        if l1.val <= l2.val:
7            current.next = l1
8            l1 = l1.next
9        else:
10            current.next = l2
11            l2 = l2.next
12        current = current.next
13
14    # Attach remaining nodes
15    current.next = l1 or l2
16    return dummy.next

4. Partition List (LC 86):

1def partition(self, head, x):
2    before_dummy = ListNode(0)
3    after_dummy = ListNode(0)
4    before = before_dummy
5    after = after_dummy
6
7    while head:
8        if head.val < x:
9            before.next = head
10            before = before.next
11        else:
12            after.next = head
13            after = after.next
14        head = head.next
15
16    # Connect the two parts
17    after.next = None
18    before.next = after_dummy.next
19    return before_dummy.next

5. Add Two Numbers (LC 2):

1def addTwoNumbers(self, l1, l2):
2    dummy = ListNode(0)
3    current = dummy
4    carry = 0
5
6    while l1 or l2 or carry:
7        val1 = l1.val if l1 else 0
8        val2 = l2.val if l2 else 0
9
10        total = val1 + val2 + carry
11        carry = total // 10
12        current.next = ListNode(total % 10)
13
14        current = current.next
15        l1 = l1.next if l1 else None
16        l2 = l2.next if l2 else None
17
18    return dummy.next

Java Implementation:

1public ListNode removeElements(ListNode head, int val) {
2    ListNode dummy = new ListNode(0);
3    dummy.next = head;
4    ListNode current = dummy;
5
6    while (current.next != null) {
7        if (current.next.val == val) {
8            current.next = current.next.next;
9        } else {
10            current = current.next;
11        }
12    }
13
14    return dummy.next;
15}

Key Benefits of Dummy Head:

Aspect Without Dummy With Dummy
Edge Cases Complex head handling Unified approach
Code Length More conditional logic Cleaner, shorter
Bug Probability Higher (edge cases) Lower (consistent)
Readability Harder to follow More intuitive

Related Problems:

  • LC 19: Remove Nth Node From End of List
  • LC 21: Merge Two Sorted Lists
  • LC 83: Remove Duplicates from Sorted List
  • LC 86: Partition List
  • LC 203: Remove Linked List Elements
  • LC 328: Odd Even Linked List

Remove Elements by Value Pattern

Definition: Remove all nodes from a linked list that match a specific value. Uses dummy head and a “look ahead” technique where the current pointer examines curr.next rather than curr itself.

Core Concept:

  • Key Insight: When we find a node to remove, we ONLY update the pointer connection (curr.next = curr.next.next), but the curr pointer itself does NOT move forward
  • This allows handling consecutive matching nodes (e.g., [6,6,6,3] with val=6)
  • Only move curr forward when curr.next.val != val

When to Use:

  • Removing nodes by value from anywhere in the list
  • Handling cases where head node(s) might need removal
  • Removing consecutive duplicate values

Time Complexity: O(n) Space Complexity: O(1)

Template Pattern:

1// Java
2public ListNode removeElements(ListNode head, int val) {
3    // 1. Create dummy node pointing to head
4    ListNode dummy = new ListNode(0);
5    dummy.next = head;
6
7    // 2. Use curr pointer (starts at dummy, looks ahead)
8    ListNode curr = dummy;
9
10    // 3. Look ahead at NEXT node
11    while (curr.next != null) {
12        if (curr.next.val == val) {
13            // Found match - skip the next node
14            // NOTE: curr does NOT move!
15            curr.next = curr.next.next;
16        } else {
17            // No match - move pointer forward
18            curr = curr.next;
19        }
20    }
21
22    // 4. Return actual head
23    return dummy.next;
24}

1# Python
2def removeElements(self, head: ListNode, val: int) -> ListNode:
3    dummy = ListNode(0)
4    dummy.next = head
5    curr = dummy
6
7    while curr.next:
8        if curr.next.val == val:
9            curr.next = curr.next.next  # skip, don't move curr
10        else:
11            curr = curr.next  # move forward
12
13    return dummy.next

Dry Run Example ([6,6,6,3], val=6):

1Initial: dummy -> 6 -> 6 -> 6 -> 3, curr at dummy
2
3Step 1: curr.next.val = 6 (match!)
4  Action: curr.next = curr.next.next
5  Result: dummy -> 6 -> 6 -> 3 (curr STAYS at dummy)
6
7Step 2: curr.next.val = 6 (match!)
8  Action: curr.next = curr.next.next
9  Result: dummy -> 6 -> 3 (curr STAYS at dummy)
10
11Step 3: curr.next.val = 6 (match!)
12  Action: curr.next = curr.next.next
13  Result: dummy -> 3 (curr STAYS at dummy)
14
15Step 4: curr.next.val = 3 (no match)
16  Action: curr = curr.next
17  Result: curr moves to node 3
18
19Step 5: curr.next = null, exit loop
20Return: dummy.next = [3]

Why This Works for Consecutive Matches:

Scenario Without “stay in place” With “stay in place”
[6,6,3] val=6 Would skip second 6 Catches all 6s
Head removal Needs special case Handled uniformly

Similar LC Problems:

  • LC 203: Remove Linked List Elements (exact pattern)
  • LC 83: Remove Duplicates from Sorted List (similar, compare adjacent)
  • LC 82: Remove Duplicates from Sorted List II (remove all duplicates)
  • LC 237: Delete Node in a Linked List (different - no access to prev)
  • LC 1474: Delete N Nodes After M Nodes (pattern variation)
  • LC 2487: Remove Nodes From Linked List (stack-based variation)

1) General form

1// java
2// single Linklist
3public class ListNode {
4    int val;
5    ListNode next;
6    ListNode(int x) { val = x; }
7}

1# python
2class Node:
3  """
4  # constructor
5  # A single node of a singly linked list
6  """
7  def __init__(self, data=None, next=None): 
8    self.data = data
9    self.next = next
10
11class LinkedList:
12  """
13  # A Linked List class with a single head node
14  """
15  def __init__(self):  
16    self.head = Node()
17
18  def get_length(self):
19    """
20    # get list length method for the linked list
21    i.e. 
22       before : 1 -> 2 -> 3
23       after  : 3
24    """
25    current = self.head
26    length = 0 
27    while current:
28        current = current.next
29        length += 1 
30    return length
31
32  def get_tail(self):
33    """
34    # get list tail method for the linked list
35    i.e. 
36       before : a -> b -> c
37       after  : c
38    """
39    current = self.head
40    while current:
41        current = current.next
42    return current
43
44  def print(self):
45    """
46    # print method for the linked list
47    i.e. 
48       before : 1 -> 2 -> 3
49       after  : 1 2 3 
50    """
51    current = self.head
52    while current:
53      print (current.data)
54      current = current.next
55
56  def append(self, data):
57    """
58    # append method that append a new item at the end of the linkedlist 
59    i.e. 
60         before :  1 -> 2 -> 3
61         after  :  1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4
62    """
63    newNode = Node(data)
64    if self.head:
65      current = self.head
66      while current.next:
67        current = current.next
68      current.next = newNode
69    else:
70      self.head = newNode
71  
72  def prepend(self, data):
73    """
74    # append method that append a new item at the head of the linkedlist 
75    i.e. 
76         before :  1 -> 2 -> 3
77         after  :  0 -> 1 -> 2 -> 3
78    """
79    newNode = Node(data)
80    if self.head:
81        current = self.head
82        self.head = newNode
83        newNode.next = current
84        current = current.next
85    else:
86        self.head = newNode
87
88  def insert(self, idx, data):
89    """
90    # append method that append a new item within the linkedlist 
91    i.e. 
92         before :  1 -> 2 -> 3
93         insert(1, 2.5)
94         after  :  1 -> 2 -> 2.5 -> 3
95         before :  1 -> 2 -> 3
96         insert(0, 0)
97         after  :  0 -> 1 -> 2 -> 3
98         before :  1 -> 2 -> 3
99         insert(2, 4)
100         after  :  1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4
101    """
102    current = self.head
103    ll_length = self.get_length()
104
105    if idx < 0 or idx > self.get_length():
106      print ("idx out of linkedlist range, idx : {}".format(idx))
107      return
108    elif idx == 0:
109        self.prepend(data)
110    elif idx == ll_length:
111        self.append(data)
112    else:
113        newNode = Node(data)
114        current = self.head
115        cur_idx = 0 
116        while cur_idx < idx-1:
117            current = current.next
118            cur_idx += 1 
119        newNode.next = current.next
120        current.next = newNode
121
122  def remove(self, idx):
123    """
124    # remove method for the linked list
125    i.e. 
126       before : 1 -> 2 -> 3
127       remove(1) 
128       after  : 1 -> 3
129       before : 1 -> 2 -> 3
130       remove(2) 
131       after  : 1 -> 2
132       before : 1 -> 2 -> 3
133       remove(0) 
134       after  : 2 -> 3
135    """
136    if idx < 0 or idx > self.get_length():
137        print ("idx out of linkedlist range, idx : {}".format(idx))
138        return 
139    elif idx == 0:
140        current = self.head
141        self.head = current.next
142    elif idx == self.get_length():
143        current = self.head
144        cur_idx = 0
145        while cur_idx < idx -1:
146            current = current.next
147            cur_idx += 1
148        current.next = None
149    else:
150        current = self.head
151        cur_idx = 0 
152        while cur_idx < idx - 1:
153            current = current.next
154            cur_idx += 1 
155        next_ = current.next.next
156        current.next = next_
157        current = next_ 
158
159  def reverse(self): 
160    """
161    https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=D7y_hoT_YZI
162
163    # reverse method for the linked list
164    # https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/python-program-for-reverse-a-linked-list/
165    i.e. 
166     before : 1 -> 2 -> 3
167     after  : 3 -> 2 -> 1 
168    """
169    prev = None
170    current = self.head 
171    while(current is not None): 
172        next_ = current.next
173        current.next = prev 
174        prev = current 
175        current = next_
176    self.head = prev 
177

1-1) Basic OP

1-1-0) Remove Nth node from end

1// java
2// LC 19
3
4// ...
5ListNode dummy = new ListNode(0);
6dummy.next = head;
7ListNode fast = dummy;
8ListNode slow = dummy;
9
10for (int i = 1; i <= n+1; i++){
11    //System.out.println("i = " + i);
12    fast = fast.next;
13}
14
15// move fast and slow pointers on the same time
16while (fast != null){
17    fast = fast.next;
18    slow = slow.next;
19}
20
21// NOTE here
22slow.next = slow.next.next;
23// ...

1-1-1) transversal linked list

1-1-2) plus one on linked list

1-1-3) Reverse linked list (iteration)

1# python
2#-------------------------
3# iteration
4#-------------------------
5# LC 206
6
7# V0
8# IDEA : Linkedlist basics
9# https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=D7y_hoT_YZI
10# STEPS)
11# -> STEP 1) cache "next"
12# -> STEP 2) point head.next to prev
13# -> STEP 3) move prev to head
14# -> STEP 4) move head to "next"
15class Solution(object):
16    def reverseList(self, head):
17        # edge case
18        if not head:
19            return
20        prev = None
21        while head:
22            # cache "next"
23            tmp = head.next
24            # point head.next to prev
25            head.next = prev
26            # move prev to head (for next iteration)
27            prev = head
28            # move head to "next" (for next iteration)
29            head = tmp
30        # NOTE!!! we return prev
31        return prev

1// java
2//---------------------------
3// iteration
4//---------------------------
5// LC 206
6// V0
7public ListNode reverseList(ListNode head) {
8
9    if (head == null) {
10        return null;
11    }
12
13    ListNode _prev = null;
14
15    while (head != null) {
16        /**
17         *  NOTE !!!!
18         *
19         *   4 operations
20         *
21         *    step 1) cache next
22         *    step 2) point cur to prev
23         *    step 3) move prev to cur
24         *    step 4) move cur to next
25         *
26         */
27        ListNode _next = head.next;
28        head.next = _prev;
29        _prev = head;
30        head = _next;
31    }
32
33    // NOTE!!! we return _prev here, since it's now "new head"
34    return _prev;
35
36}

1-1-4) Reverse linked list (recursion)

1// java
2//---------------------------
3// recursion
4//---------------------------
5// LC 206
6// algorithm book (labu) p.290
7// IDEA : Recursive
8// https://leetcode.com/problems/reverse-linked-list/editorial/
9// https://github.com/yennanliu/CS_basics/blob/master/leetcode_java/src/main/java/LeetCodeJava/LinkedList/ReverseLinkedList.java
10// same as above

1-1-5) Reverse nodes in [a,b] linked list (iteration)

1// java
2//---------------------------
3// iteration
4//---------------------------
5// algorithm book (labu) p.298
6ListNode reverse(ListNode a, Listnode b){
7    ListNode pre, cur, nxt;
8    pre = null;
9    cur = a;
10    nxt = a;
11    /** THE ONLY DIFFERENCE (reverse nodes VS reverse nodes in [a,b]) */
12    while (cur != b){
13        nxt = cur.next;
14        // reverse on each node
15        cur.next = pre;
16        // update pointer
17        pre = cur;
18        cur = nxt;
19    }
20
21    // return reversed nodes
22    return pre;
23}

1-1-6) Reverse nodes in k group linked list (iteration)

1// java
2//---------------------------
3// iteration
4//---------------------------
5// LC 25
6// algorithm book (labu) p.298
7ListNode reverse(ListNode a, Listnode b){
8    ListNode pre, cur, nxt;
9    pre = null;
10    cur = a;
11    nxt = a;
12    /** THE ONLY DIFFERENCE (reverse first N nodes VS reverse nodes in [a,b]) */
13    while (cur != b){
14        nxt = cur.next;
15        // reverse on each node
16        cur.next = pre;
17        // update pointer
18        pre = cur;
19        cur = nxt;
20    }
21
22    // return reversed nodes
23    return pre;
24}
25
26ListNode reverseKGroup(ListNode head, int k){
27    if (head == null) return null;
28    // inverval [a,b] has k to-reverse elements
29    ListNode a, b;
30    a = b = head;
31    for (int i = 0; i < k; i++){
32        // not enough elements (amount < k), no need to reverse -> base case
33        if (b == null) return head;
34        b = b.next;
35    }
36    // reverse k elements
37    ListNode newHead = reverse(a,b);
38    // reverse remaining nodes, and connect with head
39    a.next = reverseKGroup(b,k);
40    return newHead;
41}

1# LC 025
2class Solution:
3    def reverseKGroup(self, head, k):
4        # help func
5        # check if # of sub nodes still > k
6        def check(head, k):
7            ans = 0
8            while head:
9                ans += 1
10                if ans >= k:
11                    return True
12                head = head.next
13            return False
14
15        # edge case
16        if not head:
17            return
18        d = dummy = ListNode(None)
19        pre = None
20        preHead = curHead = head
21        while check(curHead, k):
22            for _ in range(k):
23                # reverse linked list
24                tmp = curHead.next
25                curHead.next = pre
26                pre = curHead
27                curHead = tmp
28            # reverse linked list
29            # ???
30            dummy.next = pre
31            dummy = preHead
32            preHead.next = curHead
33            preHead = curHead
34        return d.next

1-1-7) Reverse first N linked list (recursion)

1//---------------------------
2// recursion
3//---------------------------
4// java
5// algorithm book (labu) p.293
6
7// "postorder" node
8ListNode successor = null;
9
10// reverse first N node (from head), and return new head
11ListNode reverseN(ListNode head, int n){
12    if (n == 1){
13        // record n + 1 nodes, will be used in following steps
14        successor = head.next;
15        return head;
16    }
17
18    // set head.next as start point, return first n - 1 nodes
19    ListNode last = reverseN(head.next, n-1);
20
21    head.next.next = head;
22    // connect reversed head node and following nodes
23    head.next = successor;
24    return last;
25}

1-1-8) Reverse middle N nodes in linked list (start, end as interval) (recursion)

1// java
2//---------------------------
3// recursion
4//---------------------------
5// algorithm book (labu) p.293
6
7// "postorder" node
8ListNode successor = null;
9
10// reverse first N node (from head), and return new head
11ListNode reverseN(ListNode head, int n){
12    if (n == 1){
13        // record n + 1 nodes, will be used in following steps
14        successor = head.next;
15        return head;
16    }
17
18// reverse nodes in index = m to index = n
19ListNode reverseBetween(ListNode head, int m, int n){
20    // base case
21    if (m == 1){
22        return reverseN(head, n);
23    }
24
25    // for head.next, the op is reverse interval : [m-1, n-1]
26    // will trigger base case when when meet reverse start point
27    head.next = reverseBetween(head.next, m - 1, n - 1);
28    return head;

1-1-9) delete node

1-1-10) swap node

1-1-11) add 2 linked list

1# LC 002
2class Solution(object):
3    def addTwoNumbers(self, l1, l2):
4        """
5        NOTE :
6         1. we init linkedlist via ListNode()
7         2. we NEED make extra head refer same linkedlist, since we need to return beginning of linkedlist of this func, while res will meet "tail" at the end of while loop
8        """
9        head = res = ListNode()
10        plus = 0
11        tmp = 0
12        while l1 or l2:
13            tmp += plus
14            plus = 0
15            if l1:
16                tmp += l1.val
17                l1 = l1.next
18            if l2:
19                tmp += l2.val
20                l2 = l2.next
21            if tmp > 9:
22                tmp -= 10
23                plus = 1
24
25            res.next = ListNode(tmp)
26            res = res.next
27            tmp = 0
28        ### NOTE : need to deal with case : l1, l2 are completed, but still "remaining" plus
29        if plus != 0:
30            res.next = ListNode(plus)
31            res = res.next
32        #print ("res = " + str(res))
33        #print ("head = " + str(head))
34        return head.next

1# LC 445 Add Two Numbers II
2# V0
3# IDEA : string + linked list
4# DEMO
5# input :
6# [7,2,4,3]
7# [5,6,4]
8# intermedia output : 
9# l1_num = 7243
10# l2_num = 564
11class Solution:
12    def addTwoNumbers(self, l1, l2):
13        if not l1 and not l2:
14            return None
15
16        l1_num = 0
17        while l1:
18            l1_num = l1_num * 10 + l1.val
19            l1 = l1.next
20
21        l2_num = 0
22        while l2:
23            l2_num = l2_num * 10 + l2.val
24            l2 = l2.next
25
26        print ("l1_num = " + str(l1_num))
27        print ("l2_num = " + str(l2_num))
28
29
30        ### NOTE : trick here :
31        #    -> get int format of 2 linked list first (l1, l2)
32        #    -> then sum them (l1_num + l2_num)
33        lsum = l1_num + l2_num
34
35        head = ListNode(None)
36        cur = head
37        ### NOTE : go thrpigh the linked list int sum, append each digit to ListNode and return it
38        for istr in str(lsum):
39            cur.next = ListNode(int(istr))
40            cur = cur.next
41        # NOTE : need to return head (but not cur, since cur already meet the end of ListNode)
42        return head.next

1-1-12) Find linked list middle point

1// algorithm book p. 286
2// java
3Listnode slow, fast;
4slow = fast = head;
5while (fast && fast.next){
6    fast = fast.next.next;
7    slow = slow.next;
8}
9// slow pointer will be linked list middle point
10
11// if element count in linked list is odd (TO VERIFY)
12if (fast != null){
13    slow = slow.next;
14}

1# LC 876 Middle of the Linked List
2# V0
3# IDEA : fast, slow pointers + linkedlist
4class Solution(object):
5    def middleNode(self, head):
6        # edge case
7        if not head:
8            return
9        s = f = head
10        while f and f.next:
11            # if not f:
12            #     break
13            f = f.next.next
14            s = s.next
15        return s

2) LC Example

2-1) palindrome-linked-list

1# LC 234 : palindrome-linked-list
2# V0
3# IDEA : LINKED LIST -> LIST
4# EXAMPLE INPUT :
5# [1,2,2,1]
6# WHILE GO THROUGH :
7# head = ListNode{val: 2, next: ListNode{val: 2, next: ListNode{val: 1, next: None}}}
8# head = ListNode{val: 2, next: ListNode{val: 1, next: None}}
9# head = ListNode{val: 1, next: None}
10class Solution(object):
11    def isPalindrome(self, head):
12        ### NOTE : THE CONDITION
13        if not head or not head.next:
14            return True
15        r = []
16        ### NOTE : THE CONDITION
17        while head:
18            r.append(head.val)
19            head = head.next
20        return r == r[::-1]

2-2) Merge Two Sorted Lists

1# LC 021
2# V0
3# IDEA : LOOP 2 LINKED LISTS
4class Solution(object):
5    def mergeTwoLists(self, l1, l2):
6        if not l1 or not l2:
7            return l1 or l2
8        ### NOTICE THIS
9        #   -> we init head, and cur
10        #   -> use cur for `link` op
11        #   -> and return the `head.next`
12        head = cur = ListNode(0)
13        while l1 and l2:
14            if l1.val < l2.val:
15                """
16                ### NOTE
17                 1) assign node to cur.next !!! (not cur)
18                 2) assign node rather than node.val
19                """ 
20                cur.next = l1
21                l1 = l1.next
22            else:
23                """
24                ### NOTE
25                 1) assign node to cur.next !!! (not cur)
26                 2) assign node rather than node.val
27                """ 
28                cur.next = l2
29                l2 = l2.next
30            # note this
31            cur = cur.next
32        ### NOTE this (in case either l1 or l2 is remaining so we need to append one of them to cur)
33        cur.next = l1 or l2
34        ### NOTICE THIS : we return head.next
35        return head.next

2-2’) Merge K Sorted Lists

1# LC 023 Merge k sorted lists
2# V0
3# IDEA : LC 021 Merge Two Sorted Lists + implement mergeTwoLists on every 2 linedlist
4# linked_list.html#1-1-6-reverse-nodes-in-k-group--linked-list-iteration
5class Solution(object):
6    def mergeKLists(self, lists):
7        if len(lists) == 0:
8            return
9        if len(lists) == 1:
10            return lists[0]
11        
12        _init_list = lists[0]
13        for _list in lists[1:]:
14            tmp = self.mergeTwoLists(_init_list, _list)
15            _init_list = tmp
16        return tmp
17
18    # LC 021 : https://github.com/yennanliu/CS_basics/blob/master/leetcode_python/Linked_list/merge-two-sorted-lists.py
19    def mergeTwoLists(self, l1, l2):
20
21        if not l1 or not l2:
22            return l1 or l2
23            
24        res = head = ListNode()
25        while l1 and l2:
26            if l1.val < l2.val:
27                res.next = l1
28                l1 = l1.next
29            else:
30                res.next = l2
31                l2 = l2.next
32            res = res.next
33
34        if l1 or l2:
35            res.next = l1 or l2
36
37        return head.next

2-3) Reverse Linked List

1# LC 206
2class Solution(object):
3    def reverseList(self, head):
4        # edge case
5        if not head:
6            return
7        prev = None
8        while head:
9            # cache "next"
10            tmp = head.next
11            # point head.next to prev
12            head.next = prev
13            # move prev to head
14            prev = head
15            # move head to "next"
16            head = tmp
17        return prev

2-4) Reverse Linked List II

1// java
2
3  // V0-1
4  // IDEA: LINKED LIST OP (iteration 1)
5  // https://neetcode.io/solutions/reverse-linked-list-ii
6  // https://youtu.be/RF_M9tX4Eag?si=vTfAtfbmGwzsmtpi
7  public ListNode reverseBetween_0_1(ListNode head, int left, int right) {
8      ListNode dummy = new ListNode(0);
9      dummy.next = head;
10      ListNode leftPrev = dummy, cur = head;
11
12      for (int i = 0; i < left - 1; i++) {
13          leftPrev = cur;
14          cur = cur.next;
15      }
16
17      ListNode prev = null;
18      for (int i = 0; i < right - left + 1; i++) {
19          ListNode tmpNext = cur.next;
20          cur.next = prev;
21          prev = cur;
22          cur = tmpNext;
23      }
24
25      leftPrev.next.next = cur;
26      leftPrev.next = prev;
27
28      return dummy.next;
29  }

1# LC 92 Reverse Linked List II
2# V1
3# IDEA : Iterative Link Reversal
4# https://leetcode.com/problems/reverse-linked-list-ii/solution/
5class Solution:
6    def reverseBetween(self, head, m, n):
7
8        # Empty list
9        if not head:
10            return None
11
12        # Move the two pointers until they reach the proper starting point
13        # in the list.
14        cur, prev = head, None
15        while m > 1:
16            prev = cur
17            cur = cur.next
18            m, n = m - 1, n - 1
19
20        # The two pointers that will fix the final connections.
21        tail, con = cur, prev
22
23        # Iteratively reverse the nodes until n becomes 0.
24        while n:
25            third = cur.next
26            cur.next = prev
27            prev = cur
28            cur = third
29            n -= 1
30
31        # Adjust the final connections as explained in the algorithm
32        if con:
33            con.next = prev
34        else:
35            head = prev
36        tail.next = cur
37        return head

2-5) Copy List with Random Pointer

1# LC 138. Copy List with Random Pointer
2# V0
3# IDEA : 
4#   step 1) make 2 objects (m, n) refer to same instance (head)
5#   step 2) go through m, and set up the dict
6#   step 3) go through n, and get the random pointer via the dict we set up in step 2)
7class Node(object):
8    def __init__(self, val, next, random):
9        self.val = val
10        self.next = next
11        self.random = random
12
13class Solution:
14    def copyRandomList(self, head):
15        dic = dict()
16        ### NOTE : make m, and n refer to same instance (head)
17        m = n = head
18        while m:
19            ### NOTE : the value in dict is Node type (LinkedList)
20            dic[m] = Node(m.val)
21            m = m.next
22        while n:
23            dic[n].next = dic.get(n.next)
24            dic[n].random = dic.get(n.random)
25            n = n.next
26        return dic.get(head)

1// java
2// NOTE : there is also recursive solution
3// LC 138
4// V2
5// IDEA :  Iterative with O(N) Space
6// https://leetcode.com/problems/copy-list-with-random-pointer/editorial/
7// Visited dictionary to hold old node reference as "key" and new node reference as the "value"
8HashMap<Node, Node> visited = new HashMap<Node, Node>();
9
10public Node getClonedNode(Node node) {
11    // If the node exists then
12    if (node != null) {
13        // Check if the node is in the visited dictionary
14        if (this.visited.containsKey(node)) {
15            // If its in the visited dictionary then return the new node reference from the dictionary
16            return this.visited.get(node);
17        } else {
18            // Otherwise create a new node, add to the dictionary and return it
19            this.visited.put(node, new Node(node.val, null, null));
20            return this.visited.get(node);
21        }
22    }
23    return null;
24}
25
26public Node copyRandomList_3(Node head) {
27
28    if (head == null) {
29        return null;
30    }
31
32    Node oldNode = head;
33
34    // Creating the new head node.
35    Node newNode = new Node(oldNode.val);
36    this.visited.put(oldNode, newNode);
37
38    // Iterate on the linked list until all nodes are cloned.
39    while (oldNode != null) {
40        // Get the clones of the nodes referenced by random and next pointers.
41        newNode.random = this.getClonedNode(oldNode.random);
42        newNode.next = this.getClonedNode(oldNode.next);
43
44        // Move one step ahead in the linked list.
45        oldNode = oldNode.next;
46        newNode = newNode.next;
47    }
48    return this.visited.get(head);
49}

2-6) Intersection of Two Linked Lists

1# LC 160 Intersection of Two Linked Lists
2# V0
3# IDEA : if the given 2 linked list have intersection, then 
4#        they must overlap in SOMEWHERE if we go through
5#        each of them in the same length
6#        -> e.g.
7#             process1 : headA -> headB -> headA ...
8#             process2 : headB -> headA -> headB ...
9class Solution(object):
10    def getIntersectionNode(self, headA, headB):
11        if not headA or not headB:
12            return None
13        p, q = headA, headB
14        while p and q and p != q:
15            p = p.next
16            q = q.next
17            if p == q:
18                return p
19            if not p:
20                p = headB
21            if not q:
22                q = headA
23        return p

2-7) Split Linked List in Parts

1# LC 725. Split Linked List in Parts
2# V0
3# IDEA : LINKED LIST OP + mod op
4class Solution(object):
5    def splitListToParts(self, head, k):
6        # NO need to deal with edge case !!!
7        # get linked list length
8        _len = 0
9        _head = cur = head
10        while _head:
11            _len += 1
12            _head = _head.next
13        # init res
14        res = [None] * k
15        ### NOTE : we loop over k
16        for i in range(k):
17            """
18            2 cases
19
20            case 1) i < (_len % k) : there is "remainder" ((_len % k)), so we need to add extra 1
21                    -> _cnt_elem = (_len // k) + 1
22            case 2) i == (_len % k) : there is NO "remainder"
23                    -> _cnt_elem = (_len // k)
24            """
25            # NOTE THIS !!!
26            _cnt_elem = (_len // k) + (1 if i < (_len % k) else 0)
27            ### NOTE : we loop over _cnt_elem (length of each "split" linkedlist)
28            for j in range(_cnt_elem):
29                """
30                3 cases
31                 1) j == 0                (begin of sub linked list)
32                 2) j == _cnt_elem - 1    (end of sub linked list)
33                 3) 0 < j < _cnt_elem - 1 (middle within sub linked list)
34                """
35                # NOTE THIS !!!
36                # NOTE we need keep if - else in BELOW ORDER !!
37                #  -> j == 0, j == _cnt_elem - 1, else
38                if j == 0:
39                    res[i] = cur
40                ### NOTE this !!! : 
41                #    -> IF (but not elif)
42                #    -> since we also need to deal with j == 0 and j == _cnt_elem - 1 case
43                if j == _cnt_elem - 1:  # note this !!!
44                    # get next first
45                    tmp = cur.next
46                    # point cur.next to None
47                    cur.next = None
48                    # move cur to next (tmp) for op in next i (for i in range(k))
49                    cur = tmp
50                else:
51                    cur = cur.next
52        #print ("res = " + str(res))
53        return res

2-7) Remove Nth Node From End of List

1# LC 19. Remove Nth Node From End of List
2# NOTE : there is (two pass algorithm) approach
3# V0
4# IDEA : FAST-SLOW POINTERS (One pass algorithm)
5# IDEA :
6#   step 1) we move fast pointers n+1 steps -> so slow, fast pointers has n distance (n+1-1 == n)
7#   step 2) we move fast, and slow pointers till fast pointer meet the end
8#   step 3) then we point slow.next to slow.next.next (same as we remove n node)
9#   step 4) we return new_head.next as final result
10class Solution(object):
11    def removeNthFromEnd(self, head, n):
12        new_head = ListNode(0)
13        new_head.next = head
14        fast = slow = new_head
15        for i in range(n+1):
16            fast = fast.next
17        while fast:
18            fast = fast.next
19            slow = slow.next
20        slow.next = slow.next.next
21        return new_head.next

1// java
2    public ListNode removeNthFromEnd(ListNode head, int n) {
3
4        if (head == null){
5            return head;
6        }
7
8        if (head.next == null && head.val == n){
9            return null;
10        }
11
12        // move fast pointer only with n+1 step
13        // 2 cases:
14        //   - 1) node count is even
15        //   - 2) node count is odd
16        /** NOTE !! we init dummy pointer, and let fast, slow pointers point to it */
17        ListNode dummy = new ListNode(0);
18        dummy.next = head;
19        // NOTE here
20        ListNode fast = dummy;
21        ListNode slow = dummy;
22        /**
23         *  Explanation V1:
24         *
25         *   -> So we have fast, and slow pointer,
26         *   if we move fast N steps first,
27         *   then slow starts to move
28         *      -> fast, slow has N step difference
29         *      -> what's more, when fast reach the end,
30         *      -> fast, slow STILL has N step difference
31         *      -> and slow has N step difference with the end,
32         *      -> so we can remove N th pointer accordingly
33         *
34         *  Explanation V2:
35         *
36         *
37         *   // NOTE !!! we let fast pointer move N+1 step first
38         *   // so once fast pointers reach the end after fast, slow pointers move together
39         *   // we are sure that slow pointer is at N-1 node
40         *   // so All we need to do is :
41         *   // point slow.next to slow.next.next
42         *   // then we remove N node from linked list
43         */
44        for (int i = 1; i <= n+1; i++){
45            //System.out.println("i = " + i);
46            fast = fast.next;
47        }
48
49        // move fast and slow pointers on the same time
50        while (fast != null){
51            fast = fast.next;
52            slow = slow.next;
53        }
54
55        // NOTE here
56        slow.next = slow.next.next;
57        // NOTE !!! we return dummy.next instead of slow
58        return dummy.next;
59    }

1// java
2    // V0
3    // IDEA : get len of linkedlist, and re-point node
4    public ListNode removeNthFromEnd_0(ListNode head, int n) {
5
6        if (head.next == null){
7            return null;
8        }
9
10        // below op is optional
11//        if (head.next.next == null){
12//            if (n == 1){
13//                return new ListNode(head.val);
14//            }
15//            return new ListNode(head.next.val);
16//        }
17
18        // get len
19        int len = 0;
20        ListNode head_ = head;
21        while (head_ != null){
22            head_ = head_.next;
23            len += 1;
24        }
25
26        ListNode root = new ListNode();
27        /** NOTE !!! root_ is the actual final result */
28        ListNode root_ = root;
29
30        // if n == len
31        if (n == len){
32            head = head.next;
33            root.next = head;
34            root = root.next;
35        }
36
37        /**
38         *  IDEA: get length of linked list,
39         *        then if want to delete n node from the end of linked list,
40         *        -> then we need to stop at "len - n" idx,
41         *        -> and reconnect "len - n" idx to "len -n + 2" idx
42         *        -> (which equals delete "n" idx node
43         *
44         *
45         *  Consider linked list below :
46         *
47         *   0, 1, 2 , 3, 4 .... k-2, k-1, k
48         *
49         *   if n = 1, then "k-1" is the node to be removed.
50         *   -> so we find "k-2" node, and re-connect it to "k" node
51         */
52        /** NOTE !!!
53         *
54         *  idx is the index, that we "stop",  and re-connect
55         *  from idx to its next next node (which is the actual "delete" node op
56         */
57        int idx = len - n; // NOTE !!! this
58        while (idx > 0){
59            root.next = head;
60            root = root.next;
61            head = head.next;
62            idx -= 1;
63        }
64
65        ListNode next = head.next;
66        root.next = next;
67
68        return root_.next;
69    }

2-8) Reorder List

1// java
2    public void reorderList(ListNode head) {
3        // Edge case: empty or single node list
4        if (head == null || head.next == null) {
5            return;
6        }
7
8        // Step 1: Find the middle node
9        ListNode slow = head, fast = head;
10        while (fast != null && fast.next != null) {
11            slow = slow.next;
12            fast = fast.next.next;
13        }
14
15        // Step 2: Reverse the second half of the list
16        /** NOTE !!!
17         *
18         *  reverse on `slow.next` node
19         */
20        ListNode secondHalf = reverseNode_(slow.next);
21        /** NOTE !!!
22         *
23         *  `cut off` slow node's next nodes via point it to null node
24         *  (if not cut off, then in merge step, we will merge duplicated nodes
25         */
26        slow.next = null; // Break the list into two halves
27
28        // Step 3: Merge two halves
29        ListNode firstHalf = head;
30        while (secondHalf != null) {
31
32            // NOTE !!! cache `next node` before any op
33            ListNode _nextFirstHalf = firstHalf.next;
34            ListNode _nextSecondHalf = secondHalf.next;
35
36            // NOTE !!! point first node to second node, then point second node to first node's next node
37            firstHalf.next = secondHalf;
38            secondHalf.next = _nextFirstHalf;
39
40            // NOTE !!! move both node to `next` node
41            firstHalf = _nextFirstHalf;
42            secondHalf = _nextSecondHalf;
43        }
44    }
45
46    // Helper function to reverse a linked list
47    private ListNode reverseNode_(ListNode head) {
48        ListNode prev = null;
49        while (head != null) {
50            ListNode next = head.next;
51            head.next = prev;
52            prev = head;
53            head = next;
54        }
55        return prev;
56    }

1# LC 143. Reorder List
2# V0
3# IDEA : Reverse the Second Part of the List and Merge Two Sorted Lists
4class Solution:
5    def reorderList(self, head):
6        if not head:
7            return 
8        
9        # find the middle of linked list [Problem 876]
10        # in 1->2->3->4->5->6 find 4 
11        slow = fast = head
12        while fast and fast.next:
13            slow = slow.next
14            fast = fast.next.next 
15            
16        # reverse the second part of the list [Problem 206]
17        # convert 1->2->3->4->5->6 into 1->2->3->4 and 6->5->4
18        # reverse the second half in-place
19        prev, curr = None, slow
20        while curr:
21            tmp = curr.next
22            
23            curr.next = prev
24            prev = curr
25            curr = tmp    
26
27        # merge two sorted linked lists [Problem 21]
28        # merge 1->2->3->4 and 6->5->4 into 1->6->2->5->3->4
29        first, second = head, prev
30        while second.next:
31            tmp = first.next
32            first.next = second
33            first = tmp
34            
35            tmp = second.next
36            second.next = first
37            second = tmp
38
39# V0'
40# IDEA : Reverse the Second Part of the List and Merge Two Sorted Lists (simplified code from V1)
41class Solution:
42    def reorderList(self, head):
43        if not head:
44            return 
45        
46        # find the middle of linked list [Problem 876]
47        # in 1->2->3->4->5->6 find 4 
48        slow = fast = head
49        while fast and fast.next:
50            slow = slow.next
51            fast = fast.next.next 
52            
53        # reverse the second part of the list [Problem 206]
54        # convert 1->2->3->4->5->6 into 1->2->3->4 and 6->5->4
55        # reverse the second half in-place
56        prev, curr = None, slow
57        while curr:
58            curr.next, prev, curr = prev, curr, curr.next       
59
60        # merge two sorted linked lists [Problem 21]
61        # merge 1->2->3->4 and 6->5->4 into 1->6->2->5->3->4
62        first, second = head, prev
63        while second.next:
64            first.next, first = second, first.next
65            second.next, second = first, second.next
66
67# V0'''
68class Solution:
69    def reorderList(self, head):
70        if head is None:
71            return head
72
73        #find mid
74        slow = head
75        fast = head
76        while fast.next and fast.next.next:
77            slow = slow.next
78            fast = fast.next.next
79        mid = slow
80
81        #cut in the mid
82        left = head
83        right = mid.next
84        if right is None:
85            return head
86        mid.next = None
87
88        #reverse right half
89        cursor = right.next
90        right.next = None
91        while cursor:
92            next = cursor.next
93            cursor.next = right
94            right = cursor
95            cursor = next
96        
97        #merge left and right
98        dummy = ListNode(0)
99        while left or right:
100            if left is not None:
101                dummy.next = left
102                left = left.next
103                dummy = dummy.next
104            if right is not None:
105                dummy.next = right
106                right = right.next
107                dummy = dummy.next
108        return head

2-9) Swap Nodes in Pairs

1# LC 24. Swap Nodes in Pairs
2# V0 
3# IDEA : LINKED LIST
4# NOTE : 
5#   1) define 2 node via : n1, n2 = head.next, head.next.next
6#   2) START THE PROCESS FROM "RIGHT HAND SIDE",
7#      i.e. : n1.next = n2.next ( connect n1 to next node) -> connect n2 to n1 (n2.next = n1) -> connect dummy to n2 (head.next = n2)
8#   3) THEN MOVE HEAD FORWARD (head = n1)
9class Solution:
10    def swapPairs(self, head):
11        if not head or not head.next:
12            return head
13        dummy = ListNode(0)
14        dummy.next = head
15        head = dummy
16        while head.next and head.next.next:
17            n1, n2 = head.next, head.next.next
18            n1.next = n2.next
19            n2.next = n1
20            head.next = n2      
21            head = n1
22        return dummy.next

2-10) Plus One Linked List

1// java
2// LC 369
3// V1
4// IDEA : LINKED LIST OP (gpt)
5/**
6*  Step 1) reverse linked list
7*  Step 2) plus 1, bring `carry` to next digit if curSum > 9, ... repeat for all nodes
8*  Step 3) reverse linked list again
9*/
10public ListNode plusOne_1(ListNode head) {
11if (head == null) return new ListNode(1); // Handle edge case
12
13// Reverse the linked list
14head = reverseList(head);
15
16// Add one to the reversed list
17ListNode current = head;
18int carry = 1; // Start with adding one
19
20while (current != null && carry > 0) {
21  int sum = current.val + carry;
22  current.val = sum % 10; // Update the current node value
23  carry = sum / 10; // Calculate carry for the next node
24  if (current.next == null && carry > 0) {
25    current.next = new ListNode(carry); // Add a new node for carry
26    carry = 0; // No more carry after this
27  }
28  current = current.next;
29}
30
31// Reverse the list back to original order
32return reverseList(head);
33}
34
35// Utility to reverse a linked list
36private ListNode reverseList(ListNode head) {
37ListNode prev = null;
38ListNode current = head;
39
40while (current != null) {
41  ListNode next = current.next; // Save the next node
42  current.next = prev; // Reverse the link
43  prev = current; // Move prev forward
44  current = next; // Move current forward
45}
46
47return prev;
48}

2-11) Linked List Components

1// java
2// LC 817
3    // V1
4    // IDEA: set, linkedlist (gpt)
5    public int numComponents_1(ListNode head, int[] nums) {
6        // Convert nums array to a HashSet for O(1) lookups
7        Set<Integer> numsSet = new HashSet<>();
8        for (int num : nums) {
9            numsSet.add(num);
10        }
11
12        int count = 0;
13        boolean inComponent = false;
14
15        // Traverse the linked list
16        while (head != null) {
17            if (numsSet.contains(head.val)) {
18                // Start a new component if not already in one
19                if (!inComponent) {
20                    count++;
21                    inComponent = true;
22                }
23            } else {
24                // End the current component
25                inComponent = false;
26            }
27            head = head.next;
28        }
29
30        return count;
31    }
← Back to Cheat Sheets Edit on GitHub →
← Lc PatternMath →

CS_basics - Computer Science Fundamentals & Interview Preparation

GitHub • Documentation • Report Issues
